root_cause: OCS had certified stage runtimes but lacked a single replay-visible runtime that executed and certified the complete bounded cognition sequence.
runtime_model: OCS end-to-end runtime accepts replay-visible source context and replay-visible history inputs.
The runtime executes context assembly, cognition, replay-derived intent, memory and continuity, semantic resolution, clarification, OCS-to-PPP binding, and chain inspection.
The runtime fails closed if any certified stage fails closed.
The runtime preserves exact stage references and stage hashes for lineage inspection.
The runtime records clarification summary and deterministic operator summary.
The runtime marks all outputs as replay-visible, proposal-only, and non-authoritative.
The end-to-end hash is derived from stable source inputs and stage outcomes while preserving exact path-bearing stage hashes separately for lineage.
Replay reconstruction validates ordering, wrapper hashes, artifact hashes, returned continuity, and end-to-end hash continuity.
replay_impact: OCS now has append-only replay-visible end-to-end execution evidence.
Replay reconstruction can prove which OCS stages ran and which exact stage hashes were produced.
Identical OCS inputs produce the same end-to-end hash.
The runtime does not mutate source replay.
The runtime does not invoke PPP, providers, workers, approval, governance mutation, implementation, or execution.
